reasoning: D wins on the cleanest spread of mutually distinct broken assumptions plus two genuinely non-obvious ideas: the Negotiated Chair (two bodies must agree, comfort as shared equilibrium, furniture as couples-therapy medium) and the Last Chair (single-use, plant-it, becomes the tree that presses the next chair), whose closed leaves-to-chair-to-soil loop is the most generative concept in the set, all in tight, concrete prose. C is a close second and holds the single most original physics move, the Tensile Sling that routes weight sideways/up to walls and ceiling rather than down through legs, an axis no other response touches, paired with the boldly unsettling Reading Chair that can evict you. A is excellent and the most lavishly crafted, but its core ideas (air seat, metabolic, mycelium pet) recur in C and D, so it reads as the strong shared baseline rather than the most singular set. B trails on diversity: the Settle and Negative Chair both collapse to "form dissolves between uses," and it leaks a stray file-reading footer, though its Tithe (free chair whose real product is your body data) is its standout bold axis.
